reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissibility: it forces separation between what is directly supported at 100% SOC versus what cannot be extrapolated across SOC, while also leveraging a second SOC-dependent observation stream (valve opening temperature and onset temperatures). Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible, pushing experts to avoid causal overclaims and cross-condition generalizations, though some options vary in scope/strength which can slightly cue test-wise elimination.

The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissibility judgments about what can and cannot be inferred from rate-dependent warning dynamics and explicit scale-transfer limits, yielding meaningful expert discrimination. Decoys are largely single-fault and plausibly engineered, forcing careful separation of rate-invariant thresholds from rate-dependent severity and of cell results from module deployment. Minor neutrality risk comes from one option’s compound/hedged phrasing that slightly increases ambiguity and may cue test-taking rather than pure technical inference.

The item cleanly targets a single mechanism-based judgment: why stress transitions are earlier indicators than voltage/temperature, why the signal remains informative without runaway, and how conservative thresholding trades missed detections for false alarms. Boundaries are largely tight via explicit timing and SOC conditions plus must-include elements, but it remains a short-answer item with some room for plausible alternative mechanistic phrasing and threshold-justification interpretations, slightly reducing decoy/diagnostic purity compared to forced-choice formats.
